attachments.filename: JIRA Agentic Framework – Functional Design Document.pdf
content: JIRA Agentic Framework – Functional Design Document 1. Overview The JIRA Agentic Framework automates the extraction, analysis, enhancement, generation, and validation of JIRA requirements using AI-powered agents. The framework processes JIRA tickets, enriches the extracted information, generates high-quality test cases, performs quality reviews, and produces standardized JSON outputs that can be directly integrated with TestRail or other test management platforms. 2. Objectives The framework aims to: • Automatically extract complete information from JIRA issues. • Traverse and analyze referenced links and related documents. • Extract attachments and contextual comments. • Generate enhanced use case summaries. • Produce comprehensive test cases. • Review and validate generated test cases. • Assign complexity levels and identify quality gaps. • Export all outputs in standardized JSON format. 3. Agent Workflow JIRA ID │ ▼ JIRA Extraction Agent │ ▼ Use Case Summarizer Agent │ ▼ Test Case Generator Agent │ ▼ Test Case Review Agent │ 1

▼ JSON Output │ ▼ Ready for TestRail Upload 4. Agent Specifications 4.1 JIRA Extraction Agent Purpose Collect all relevant information from a given JIRA issue and enrich the requirement by gathering supporting artifacts. Responsibilities • Extract all available JIRA fields. • Extract issue description. • Extract acceptance criteria. • Extract labels. • Extract components. • Extract priority. • Extract status. • Extract assignee. • Extract reporter. • Extract issue type. • Extract epic and linked issues. • Extract custom fields. Link Scraping The agent should: • Identify all URLs referenced inside the JIRA ticket. • Scrape the content of each referenced page. • Traverse one additional reference level. • Capture meaningful business content. • Ignore duplicate references. 2

Attachment Processing Extract content from supported attachment types: • PDF • Word Documents • Excel • Images (OCR) • Text files • Markdown files Comment Extraction Extract only comments relevant to: • Requirement clarification • Scope updates • Functional discussions • Business decisions • Acceptance criteria modifications Exclude: • Status updates • Deployment notifications • Generic acknowledgements Output Format { "jiraId": "", "summary": "", "description": "", "acceptanceCriteria": [], "fields": {}, "attachments": [], "comments": [], "referencedDocuments": [] } 3

4.2 Use Case Summarizer Agent Purpose Generate a clear and complete business use case using extracted JIRA information. Responsibilities • Summarize functional requirements. • Identify business objectives. • Improve readability. • Fill missing contextual information where possible. • Generate structured business flow. • Provide supporting references. Output { "useCase": { "title": "", "objective": "", "businessFlow": [], "preConditions": [], "postConditions": [], "assumptions": [], "references": [] } } 4.3 Test Case Generator Agent Purpose Generate comprehensive functional test cases from the summarized use case. Responsibilities • Analyze business flow. • Generate positive test cases. • Generate negative test cases. • Generate boundary condition tests. • Generate validation scenarios. 4

• Map test cases to requirements. • Assign priorities. Output { "testCases": [ { "id": "", "title": "", "preConditions": [], "steps": [], "expectedResult": "", "priority": "", "requirementReference": "" } ] } 4.4 Test Case Review Agent Purpose Review generated test cases for completeness, quality, and execution readiness. Inputs • Use Case • Generated Test Cases Responsibilities Quality Validation Validate: • Requirement coverage • Missing scenarios • Duplicate test cases • Invalid test steps • Missing expected results • Missing preconditions • Inconsistent data 5

Red Flag Detection Raise warnings for: • Missing requirements • Missing negative scenarios • Missing boundary validations • Duplicate scenarios • Incomplete expected results • Low requirement coverage Complexity Classification Assign complexity based on effort: Complexity Criteria Low (L) Simple workflow, few validations Medium (M) Multiple business rules and validations High (H) Complex workflows, integrations, dependencies Completion Status Return one of: • Passed • Review Required • Incomplete Output { "review": { "qualityStatus": "Passed", "coveragePercentage": 98, "redFlags": [], "complexity": "Medium", "recommendation": "Ready for TestRail Upload" } } 6

5. Final JSON Output The framework combines outputs from all agents into a single JSON document. { "jiraExtraction": {}, "useCase": {}, "testCases": [], "review": { "qualityStatus": "", "complexity": "", "coveragePercentage": "", "redFlags": [], "recommendation": "" }, "status": "Ready for TestRail Upload" } 6. End-to-End Process Flow 1. User provides a JIRA ID. 2. JIRA Extraction Agent retrieves all issue details, attachments, comments, and referenced documents. 3. Use Case Summarizer Agent enhances and structures the business use case. 4. Test Case Generator Agent creates comprehensive test cases in JSON format. 5. Test Case Review Agent validates quality, identifies risks, assigns complexity (High/Medium/Low), and verifies completeness. 6. The framework generates a consolidated JSON output that is ready for direct import into TestRail. 7. Expected Deliverables • Complete JIRA metadata extraction. • Attachment and reference content extraction. • Enhanced business use case. • AI-generated functional test cases. • Automated quality assessment. • Complexity classification (H/M/L). • Red flag identification. • Consolidated JSON output. • TestRail-ready payload for seamless integration. 7
